task: I'm starting to rethink the design of gossamer. What are the pros and cons of packaging this app as a vscode plugin instead of a desktop app?

The pain point is install friction. Currently, gossamer needs the entire cli enabled on repos, and you need to download the app and add the repos to the app. If it's a vscode plugin, it can just test the open directories and see which ones have entire enabled. It's also easier to go back and forth between editing code and looking at the claude session.
user_feedback_text: The pain point is install friction. Currently, gossamer needs the entire cli enabled on repos, and you need to download the app and add the repos to the app. If it's a vscode plugin, it can just test the open directories and see which ones have entire enabled. It's also easier to go back and forth between editing code and looking at the claude session.

task: Let's wire up the next view, clicking on one of the session rows should open a new tab in the gossamer extension and show the sessionDetails page

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wanted to refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension. Key motivations: (1) install friction — currently requires the entire CLI enabled on repos + downloading the app + adding repos manually; (2) easier context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude sessions. The user clarified that Entire.io writes session data to a git branch, gossamer reads that and indexes it into SQLite, so the VS Code extension only needs to detect `.entire/settings.json` in workspace folders. The user explicitly said: break the Electron app if needed, keep UI as-is for now, expose only module API (no CLI) for the core package. Most recently, the user asked to wire up clicking a session row to open a SessionDetail view in a new tab within the extension.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **npm workspaces monorepo**: Root `package.json` with `"workspaces": ["packages/*", "app"]`
   - **`packages/core` (`@gossamer/core`)**: Extracted indexing logic — `indexer.ts`, `db.ts`, `search.ts`, `gitUtils.ts`, Prisma schema
   - **`packages/vscode`**: VS Code extension with two build targets — extension host (tsc/NodeNext ESM) and webview (Vite/React)
   - **Extension host**: Spawns `dist/serve.js` as a child process using system Node.js (not VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js) to avoid native module version mismatches
   - **Webview communication**: HTTP/WebSocket to the spawned gossamer server; port injected as `window.__GOSSAMER_PORT__`
   - **Server readiness**: Extension host polls `GET /api/sessions` until server responds, then posts `{ type: "server_ready" }` to webview
   - **Prisma 7 + better-sqlite3**: Native module; must run on system Node.js, not VS Code's Node.js (NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch was the key bug)
   - **Vite for webview**: Builds React + Man
Instead of changing the view in the same tab, i want you to create a new tab within vscode to show the selected sessionDetails. I also want to be able to pop out any tabs into their own windows/
user_feedback_text: Instead of changing the view in the same tab, i want you to create a new tab within vscode to show the selected sessionDetails. I also want to be able to pop out any tabs into their own windows/
